blockingIssues.description: Helper `toolContext` recursively calls itself (`toolContext(worktree)`), creating infinite recursion/stack overflow when invoked.
Schema bridge typing was narrowed to `unknown` while fluent method chaining remained, introducing a strict typecheck regression.
Test uses a machine-specific absolute path (`/Users/vriesd/...`), making the fixture parity test non-portable.
Contract test uses a machine-specific absolute repo root, so scans can become vacuous outside that exact checkout path.
Fixture file reads are path-bound to `/Users/vriesd/projects/flow-opencode`, breaking portability of duplicate-fixture enforcement.

appliedUpdates.description: Added `commands.format_check: bunx biome format --check .` so format-check assertions have an explicit shared command.
Added a portability gotcha to `.factory/library/environment.md` documenting that tests must avoid machine-specific absolute repository paths.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a maintenance/lint-only exception path: when a feature is explicitly non-behavioral cleanup, allow skipping mandatory red-first test authoring while still requiring relevant validators.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Observed in m1-tsconfig-strict, m1-biome-adoption, m1-check-green, and m1-fixture-dedup reviews where workers recorded repeated deviations against the current mandatory red-first step.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify policy for pre-existing baseline validator failures (for example lint/deadcode already failing before feature work): require explicit attribution and evidence instead of forcing strict pass/fail ambiguity.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Observed in m1-fix-typecheck-regression and m1-biome-adoption reviews where handoffs documented pre-existing validator failures conflicting with strict skill wording.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit convention forbidding machine-specific absolute repository paths in tests; require worktree-relative path resolution.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Blocking failures in m1-bench-harness and m1-fixture-dedup both stemmed from hard-coded `/Users/vriesd/projects/flow-opencode/...` test paths.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require updating the 'Known Pre-Existing Issues' section when baseline toolchain/harness issues are discovered so later workers and validators do not repeatedly rediscover them.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews reported stale/empty Known Pre-Existing Issues while handoffs referenced recurring baseline validator/harness anomalies.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add fallback command variants for every `bun run <script>` in services.yaml to account for wrapper behavior.
Record a recurring Bun test harness hang as an established known issue.
rejectedObservations.reason: not reproducible in this scrutiny run (`bun test` passed cleanly), so evidence is currently insufficient.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document lint scope narrowing (`src tests bench`) as a mission-wide convention.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in code (`package.json` script) and not yet shown to be a broad systemic policy gap.
